sections.paragraph:
συντόμως adv. of σύντομος, cp. συντέμνω (Aeschyl., Hippocr.+. Loanw. in rabb.)

① in a short time, promptly, readily (Aeschyl., Hippocr. et al.; pap; Pr 13:23; 3 Macc 5:25; Jos., Ant. 2, 315) 2 Cl 20:4; IRo 5:2; alternative short ending of Mk.

② pert. to making someth. short, briefly, concisely of discourse (Aeschyl., Isocr.; cp. Jos., C. Ap. 1, 3; 29; Just., adv. of σύντομος, cp. συντέμνω (Aeschyl., Hippocr.+. Loanw. in rabb.)

① in a short time, promptly, readily (Aeschyl., Hippocr. et al.; pap; Pr 13:23; 3 Macc 5:25; Jos., Ant. 2, 315) 2 Cl 20:4; IRo 5:2; alternative short ending of Mk.

② pert. to making someth. short, briefly, concisely of discourse (Aeschyl., Isocr.; cp. Jos., C. Ap. 1, 3; 29; Just., Tat., Ath.) IMg 14. ἀκοῦσαί τινος συντόμως give someone a hearing briefly (i.e. someone who promises to speak briefly and to the point) Ac 24:4.—DELG s.v. τέμνω. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

someth. someth. = something

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 976
